methods.description: Process for collecting zooplankton samples from the southwestern shelf water of India using a multi-plankton net.

steps.description: Zooplankton samples were collected onboard RV Sindhu Sadhana (SSD-068), during December 2019 from three locations (i.e.St 1-11.002°N, 75.1159°E, St 2-14°N, 73.33°E, and St 3-16.0028°N, 72.6179°E) along the southwestern shelf water of India (eastern Arabian Sea).
steps.searchable_summary: collect zooplankton samples from specified locations using RV Sindhu Sadhana

steps.description: Zooplankton samples were collected with the help of a multi-plankton net (Multinet, Hydro-bios, Kiel, Germany; mouth area of 0.25 m2 and mesh size of 200 μm) operated through the mechanical winch covering the upper 200 m water depth by vertical hauls of plankton net.
steps.searchable_summary: use multi-plankton net to collect samples from upper 200 m water depth

steps.description: Immediately after collection, preserve samples with 10% formaldehyde in a clean plastic bottle till further analysis.

methods.description: Microscopic analysis of zooplankton samples to obtain photomicrographs and measurements of diatom and copepod species.

steps.description: Microscopic analysis was carried out with the help of a stereo zoom microscope (make-Nikon; Model-SMZ-18) as well as an inverted microscope (Make-Nikon; Model-Eclipse Ti2).
steps.searchable_summary: perform microscopic analysis using stereo zoom and inverted microscopes

steps.description: Photomicrographs, measurements of diatom (n = 50), and copepod were obtained by using a compound microscope (Make-Nikon; Model-Eclipse Ci).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain photomicrographs and measurements using compound microscope

steps.description: The copepod (D. affinis McMurrich 1916) was identified using the identification keys by Kang et al. (1990) and Radhika Nandan (2020).
steps.searchable_summary: identify copepod species using identification keys

steps.description: The terminology and systematic position of diatom (P. pacificum Hustedt and Krasske 1941) followed Krasske (1941), Simonsen (1970), Gibson (1978Gibson ( , 1979, Hiromi et al. (1985), Rivera et al. (1986), Round et al. (1990), Lee et al. (1993), Garate-Lizárraga and Muñeton-Gomez (2009).
steps.searchable_summary: determine diatom terminology and systematic position using literature
